8.2 Squares and Rectangles
Now, let us look at some basic figures having straight lines in their boundary.
What shapes are t these? Yes, these are our
familiar squares and rectangles. But what makes them squares and rectangles?
Consider this rectangle ABCD.
The points A, B, C and D are the corners of the rectangle. Lines AB, BC, CD and DA its sides. Its angles A, ZB, C are are and ZD.
The blue sides AB and CD are called sides, they lie opposite as opposite to each other. Likewise,A AD and BC C is the other pair of c opposite sides.
